title: Enchanted Masquerade Chess — Shader Spell Forge
description: A venetian mask floats above a chess board, acting as a mystical conduit. The user selects chess pieces to 'enchant' them with custom shader effects channeled through the mask. Each enchantment changes the mask's shader in tandem, creating a visual link between mask and piece through dissolve, glow, refraction, and chromatic aberration effects controlled via a parameter panel.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that loads venice_mask.glb floating above and centered over ABeautifulGame.glb (chess set). Use GLTFLoader from CDN. Camera: PerspectiveCamera at (0,4,6) looking at origin. Lighting: one warm DirectionalLight, one cool HemisphereLight, and a PointLight near the mask that changes color per enchantment. OrbitControls enabled.

Core shader system: Create a custom ShaderMaterial library with 5 named enchantments: (1) 'Dissolve' — Perlin noise-based dissolve with glowing edges, uniform uProgress 0-1, (2) 'Phantom Glow' — Fresnel-based rim glow with uniform uGlowColor and uIntensity, (3) 'Refraction Warp' — screen-space UV distortion simulating glass refraction with uRefractionStrength, (4) 'Chromatic Split' — RGB channel offset in screen space with uOffset, (5) 'Void Pulse' — radial pulsing darkness from object center with uPulseSpeed and uRadius. All shaders must use proper vertex and fragment shader code with at least one animated uniform driven by elapsed time.

Interaction flow (6-10 steps with validation): Display an HTML overlay panel. Step 1: User clicks a chess piece via raycasting — validate a mesh from ABeautifulGame is selected (highlight it). Step 2: User picks an enchantment from a dropdown (5 options). Step 3-5: A slider panel appears showing 2-3 uniforms specific to that enchantment; user must adjust each slider (validate each changed from default). Step 6: User clicks 'Cast Enchantment' button — the selected shader applies to BOTH the chess piece AND the venice_mask simultaneously with a brief transition animation. The mask shader should mirror the piece but with inverted or complementary parameter values. Step 7: Validate final render by checking all uniforms are within valid ranges. Show a success toast. Track enchanted pieces with a counter badge. Ensure at least 2 pieces must be enchanted with different effects before showing a 'Masquerade Complete' banner. All parameter changes must be validated (numeric, within min/max range) before applying.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  selectedPieceIndex: number,         // index of currently selected chess piece (-1 if none)
  activeEnchantmentId: number,         // ID of enchantment applied to selected piece (0-4, -1 if none)
  maskShaderType: number,         // current shader type on mask (0-4 matching enchantment)
  enchantedPieceCount: number,         // total number of pieces that have been enchanted
  pointLightColorHue: number,         // hue value (0-360) of the PointLight near mask
  dissolveProgress: number,         // dissolve shader progress uniform (0.0-1.0)
  glowIntensity: number,         // phantom glow shader intensity uniform (0.0-5.0)
  refractionStrength: number,         // refraction warp distortion strength (0.0-0.5)
  chromaticOffset: number,         // chromatic split RGB offset amount (0.0-0.05)
  voidPulseRadius: number,         // void pulse effect radius (0.0-2.0)
  animationTimeElapsed: number,         // total elapsed time in seconds for shader animations
  isOverlayVisible: boolean,         // whether the enchantment selection overlay panel is visible
}
